subject: [PATCH v1 04/17] fuse: implement passthrough for readdir

2026-04-20.patch_summary: This patch implements passthrough for readdir operations in the FUSE file system. It allows a directory to be opened in passthrough mode, where the client requests the server to perform the readdir operation instead of the kernel. This is done by setting the FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH flag when opening the directory and requesting the READDIR inode operation. The patch also ensures that a directory cannot be opened for both cached readdir and passthrough at the same time. The opt-in nature of passthrough means that there will be no READDIRPLUS call to the server, which can result in performance overhead for certain use cases like ls -l.
